title: Why “China First” Will Fail
gist: In the readable opening of this otherwise paywalled essay, Kim argues that as the US abandons its post-WWII role as guarantor of the international order under 'America first', the world is watching whether Beijing steps up, with views of China growing more favorable among traditional US allies.

insights.claim: Under 'America first', Washington is abandoning responsibility for sustaining the post-WWII international order it built and guaranteed for nearly eight decades.
insights.speaker: Patricia M. Kim
insights.so_what: A US retreat creates an order vacuum that reshapes global alignment and risk premia; the central question is whether China fills it.
insights.quote: Washington is abandoning responsibility for sustaining the system it built after World War II

insights.claim: In countries long allied with the United States, views of China are becoming more favorable, per a Politico survey conducted in February 2026.
insights.speaker: Patricia M. Kim
insights.so_what: Soft-power realignment toward Beijing among US allies is an early signal of shifting global influence as the US pulls back.
insights.quote: In countries long allied with the United States, views of China are becoming more favorable.
